item.title: Chaitra Sankranti celebrated with festivity
item.description: The Bengali nation celebrated the traditional Chaitra Sankranti festival on Monday, marking the last day of the Bangla year....

item.title: Prof Yearul Kabir appointed new JUST VC without search committee recommendation
item.description: The education ministry on Monday appointed Professor Yearul Kabir as the vice-chancellor of Jashore University of Science and Technology....

item.title: Dhaka, Jakarta for enhanced economic cooperation
item.description: Dhaka and Jakarta in a bilateral meeting in the Indonesian capital on Monday underlined the need for greater engagement between the private sectors to deepen economic cooperation between the two nations....Geographic Reference

item.title: Jobs for retired judges detrimental to judiciary: minister
item.description: Law justice and parliamentary affairs minister Md Asaduzzaman on Monday said that post-retirement jobs or incentives for judges could undermine judicial integrity and weaken institutions like the Supreme Court....

item.title: Learning opportunities for every child
item.description: EDUCATION is widely recognised as a human right and a powerful tool for changing the world. When delivered effectively, it equips people with the skills needed to participate constructively in civic life as well as contribute meaningfully to national......

item.title: INDIGENOUS PEOPLES: Self-identification, recognition, sovereignty
item.description: ‘WHAT is in a name?’ asks sociologist Eva Gerharz, borrowing from Shakespeare’s classic play Romeo and Juliet while she tries to understand the debates around indigenous identity in Bangladesh. The past three decades have been filled with a debate......

item.title: Cornered by humans, killed by crocodile
item.description: THE killing of a stray dog in the pond of Khan Jahan Ali Mazar has been widely circulated, briefly debated, and then softened into administrative routine. A video shows the animal dragged underwater by a crocodile as people stand around, many......Politics analysis

item.title: BNP’S REFORM RETREAT: Crisis of credibility in making
item.description: AFTER many years of portraying itself as the only realistic democratic option for the country, the Bangladesh Nationalist Party has found itself facing a mounting and possibly detrimental problem of credibility. Such an outcome seems quite ironic, given the high hopes......

item.title: Govt inaction that undermines solar irrigation projects
item.description: THE failure of publicly funded solar irrigation projects reflects a deep crisis of maintenance, accountability and institutional follow-through. Reports on solar irrigation schemes in Manikganj show how weaknesses have undermined an initiative once intended to......

item.title: Strong legal handles must to deal with online scams
item.description: AN INCREASE in online scams highlights critical gaps in digital regulation and citizens’ protection. Fraudulent activities include fake part-time job offers circulated on messaging platforms, sham stock investment apps, phishing through one-time passwords, parcel......

item.title: Rosenior admits Chelsea in ‘difficult place’
item.description: Liam Rosenior admitted Chelsea are in a ‘difficult place’ after Sunday’s dismal 3-0 defeat against Manchester City piled pressure on the Blues boss....

item.title: City must respect Arsenal in title showdown: Guardiola
item.description: Pep Guardiola insisted Manchester City must respect Arsenal when they face the Premier League leaders in a seismic title showdown next weekend....

item.title: Liverpool seek ‘special’ Anfield night
item.description: Liverpool have a mountain to climb to oust holders Paris Saint-Germain from the Champions League but are hoping the Anfield factor can salvage a bitterly disappointing season....

item.title: Ghana footballer dies after robbery attack on team bus
item.description: A Ghanaian Premier League footballer, Dominic Frimpong, has been killed after armed robbers attacked his team on the way back from an away fixture on Sunday, the Ghana Football Association said Monday....

item.title: DPL players’ transfer on April 18, 19
item.description: The players’ transfer for the Dhaka Premier Division Cricket League for the 2025-26 season would be held on April 18 and 19, announced the Cricket Committee of Dhaka Metropolis of the Bangladesh Cricket Board on Monday. ...Geographic Reference

item.title: Atletico need ‘personality’ to prevent Barca
item.description: Atletico Madrid midfielder Koke said Monday his team must play with ‘personality’ to stop Barcelona mounting a Champions League quarter-final comeback....

item.title: Iran releases assets of football captain
item.description: Iran’s judiciary said Monday authorities had released the assets of the captain of Iranian women’s football team which had been seized after she made and then withdrew an asylum claim in Australia last month....

item.title: 9 rescued after boat capsizes in Bay on way to Malaysia
item.description: Bangladesh Coast Guard received at least nine people, including four Rohingyas on their way to Malaysia after a Bangladeshi ship rescued them following an incident of boat capsize near Andaman Islands in the Bay of Bengal....Bangladesh travel

item.title: Dhaka urges New Delhi to ensure its territory not used to destabilise Bangladesh: adviser
item.description: Foreign affairs adviser to the prime minister Humayun Kabir said that Dhaka had urged New Delhi to ensure that Indian territory was not used for any activities against Bangladesh, in remarks linked to concerns over deposed prime minister Sheikh Hasina......

item.title: Fuel pass introduced to five more pumps
item.description: The government on Monday expanded the fuel oil pass facility to five more filling stations in the capital to provide motorcyclists with octane and petrol....

item.title: BMU physician Chandan Kumar Roy dies
item.description: Chandan Kumar Roy, associate professor of the Department of Microbiology and Immunology, Bangladesh Medical University, has passed away....

item.title: Ukraine, Russia renew drone strikes after Easter truce
item.description: Ukraine and Russia renewed overnight drone strikes on Monday, after the end of a 32-hour Orthodox Easter truce marred by accusations of mass violations, both countries said....Geographic Reference

item.title: ASEAN urges Iran peace talks, reopening of Hormuz
item.description: ASEAN encouraged the United States and Iran on Monday to press ahead with talks to end the Middle East war and ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz....

item.title: ICT allows investigators to interrogate ex-DGFI chief Mamun
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2 on Monday allowed the Investigation Agency to quiz detained former DGFI director general Sheikh Mamun Khaled in custody for two days in a crimes against humanity case linked to enforced disappearances....

item.title: Govt misleads JS over repeal of ords, accuse ex-NHRC commissioners
item.description: Five recently retired commissioners of the National Human Rights Commission on Monday in an open letter accused the government of misleading the Jatiya Sangsad to justify repealing key ordinances on enforced disappearance and the human rights commission....

item.title: ACC urged to investigate graft allegation against Yunus, ex-health adviser
item.description: A Supreme Court lawyer on Monday submitted an application to the Anti-Corruption Commission, seeking a probe into alleged corruption by former interim government chief adviser Professor Muhammad Yunus and health adviser Nurjahan Begum......

item.title: Govt fails to check vehicle modification, overloading
item.description: Modification and overloading of vehicles are continuing unabated, damaging the country’s roads and highways and causing fatal road accidents amid lax monitoring by the authorities concerned....Human Rights

item.title: 25 YEARS OF RAMNA BLAST: No end to cry for justice
item.description: Twenty-five years after the Ramna Batamul bomb attack, justice remains elusive, as the case under the Explosive Substances Act has been stuck in a lower court for the past four years, while the murder case is still awaiting final disposal in the legal process....

item.title: Pahela Baishakh today
item.description: The nation celebrates Pahela Baishakh, the first day of the Bengali New Year 1433, today by organising various programmes at public venues in Dhaka and elsewhere across the country....

item.title: Staff shortage keeps 50pc ICU beds idle
item.description: A massive expansion of intensive care infrastructure in Bangladesh following the Covid-19 pandemic has failed to translate into lifesaving services, with most of the country’s installed intensive care unit beds lying unused mainly for manpower shortage......
